features.description: Shows road type, contractor details, allocated budget, and scheduled/completed repair dates for any queried road segment.

features.feature_name: Complaint Reporting with GPS + Image Upload
features.description: Citizens submit road damage reports with auto-captured GPS coordinates and photo evidence, stored with timestamp metadata.

features.feature_name: AI Chatbot with RAG + Vector DB
features.description: Conversational chatbot answers road-related queries using Retrieval-Augmented Generation over a vector database of road records, policies, and complaints.
Public-facing dashboard showing budget allocations, contractor performance, complaint resolution rates, and project timelines.
Users get road info and complaints relevant only to their current geographic zone, reducing noise and improving relevance.
Progressive Web App with service workers caches road data and allows complaint drafting offline; syncs when connectivity is restored.
Multi-language support and adaptable data schemas to accommodate road governance structures across different countries.

Uploaded complaint images are analyzed by a computer vision model that auto-classifies damage severity (Low / Medium / Critical) and estimates repair urgency score. This score directly influences complaint routing priority.

Complaints are automatically routed to the correct government department, contractor, or municipal body based on road type, jurisdiction, and severity — with escalation rules if unacknowledged within SLA windows. Citizens receive real-time status updates via SMS/email.

ML model trained on historical repair data, weather patterns, traffic load, and road age predicts which road segments are likely to deteriorate in the next 30–90 days, enabling proactive maintenance before complaints arise.

Each contractor gets a public trust score calculated from: repair durability (re-complaint rate), on-time delivery, budget adherence, and citizen ratings. Score is visible on the dashboard and influences future contract eligibility.

Aggregates anonymized complaint data and user-reported conditions into a live heatmap overlay on the map. Darker zones indicate higher complaint density, giving citizens and officials an at-a-glance view of problem areas.

Full voice interaction with the AI chatbot — users can report complaints, ask road queries, and navigate the app entirely by voice. Supports regional accents and low-literacy users. Critical for rural and accessibility-impaired populations.

Automated auditing layer that flags statistical anomalies in budget spending — e.g., a road segment billed 3x the regional average, or repeated repairs on the same stretch within short intervals — and surfaces them on the transparency dashboard as 'Flagged for Review'.

A standardized API gateway that normalizes road data from heterogeneous government sources across countries (different schemas, formats, languages) into a unified RoadWatch data model. Enables cross-border road condition comparisons and policy benchmarking.

Every complaint submission, status update, and resolution action is recorded on a lightweight blockchain or append-only ledger, making the complaint lifecycle tamper-proof. Citizens can verify their complaint was not silently deleted or altered.

features.feature_name: Multilingual RAG with Local Governance Context
features.description: The RAG pipeline is extended with country-specific and language-specific document corpora — local road laws, municipal bylaws, contractor regulations — so the chatbot gives legally accurate, jurisdiction-aware answers in the user's native language.
